description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface, creating a striped pattern. The grooves are deep and regular, giving the block a ribbed appearance from above. The sides of the block are solid and smooth, with a dark coloration that contrasts with the lighter, textured top. The block appears dense and heavy, likely made from a type of granite or concrete, and is designed to serve as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment. The overall finish is matte and slightly rough, suggesting durability and resistance to weathering. There are no visible engravings, markings, or signs of significant wear on the surfaces.
description: A heavy, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface and smooth, dark sides. The block has a ribbed, striped appearance and a durable, matte finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the block and the parallel grooves running horizontally across the top.
description_view_1: Side-angled view highlighting the depth and spacing of the grooves, with the block's length extending back.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the same horizontal grooves and solid sides.
description_view_3: Opposite side-angled view, again emphasizing the groove depth and the block's rectangular form.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a traffic or pedestrian barrier
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set flush with pavement
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; surfaces appear clean and well-maintained.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
